Configurations
  • Tools
  • View page source

Tools

This section documents configuration for supporting tools used across the system.

Overview

The tools included here provide:

  • System information display

  • Terminal session management

Currently configured tools:

  • fastfetch

  • tmux

fastfetch

Configuration files for fastfetch are located in:

  • tools/fastfetch/

These define different output styles depending on context, such as:

  • Compact output

  • Detailed reports

  • Styled or themed layouts

Examples include:

  • small.jsonc

  • medium.jsonc

  • big-fancy.jsonc

  • report.jsonc

Default Configuration

 1// Load with --load-config examples/2.jsonc
 2// Note that you must replace the image path to an existing image to display it.
 3
 4{
 5    "$schema": "https://github.com/fastfetch-cli/fastfetch/raw/dev/doc/json_schema.json",
 6    // "logo": {
 7    //     "type": "iterm",
 8    //     "source": "~/Desktop/apple1.png",
 9    //     "width": 28,
10    //     "height": 12
11    // },
12    "display": {
13        "separator": "  ",
14        "constants": [
15            "─────────────────" // {$1}, used in Custom module
16        ],
17        "key": {
18            "type": "icon",
19            "paddingLeft": 2
20        }
21    },
22    "modules": [
23        {
24            "type": "custom", // HardwareStart
25            // {#1} is equivalent to `\u001b[1m`. {#} is equivalent to `\u001b[m`
26            "format": "┌{$1} {#1}Hardware Information{#} {$1}┐"
27        },
28        "host",
29        "cpu",
30        "gpu",
31        //"disk",
32        "memory",
33        "swap",
34        "display",
35        "brightness",
36        "battery",
37        "poweradapter",
38        "bluetooth",
39        "sound",
40        "gamepad",
41        {
42            "type": "custom", // SoftwareStart
43            "format": "├{$1} {#1}Software Information{#} {$1}┤"
44        },
45        {
46            "type": "title",
47            "keyIcon": "",
48            "key": "Title", // Title module has no key by default, so that icon is not displayed
49            "format": "{user-name}@{host-name}"
50        },
51        "os",
52        "kernel",
53        "lm",
54        "de",
55        "wm",
56        "shell",
57        "terminal",
58        "terminalfont",
59        //"theme",
60        "icons",
61        "wallpaper",
62        "packages",
63        "uptime",
64        "media",
65        //{
66        //    "type": "localip",
67        //    "compact": true
68        //},
69        //{
70        //    "type": "publicip",
71        //    "timeout": 1000
72        //},
73        //{
74        //    "type": "wifi",
75        //    "format": "{ssid}"
76        //},
77        "locale",
78        {
79            "type": "custom", // InformationEnd
80            "format": "└{$1}──────────────────────{$1}┘"
81        },
82        {
83            "type": "colors",
84            "paddingLeft": 2,
85            "symbol": "circle"
86        }
87    ]
88}

Note

The configuration files use JSONC (JSON with comments), which is supported by fastfetch.

Usage

Different configurations can be selected manually, for example:

fastfetch --config path/to/config.jsonc

tmux

The tmux configuration is defined in:

  • tools/.tmux.conf

 1# Prefix
 2unbind C-b
 3set -g prefix C-Space
 4bind C-Space send-prefix
 5
 6# General
 7set -g mode-keys vi
 8set -g status-keys vi
 9set -g default-terminal "foot"
10set -a terminal-features 'foot:RGB'
11set -g base-index 1
12set -g pane-base-index 1
13set-window-option -g pane-base-index 1
14set -g renumber-windows on
15set -sg escape-time 0
16set -g focus-events on
17set -g set-clipboard on
18
19# General binds
20bind-key h select-pane -L
21bind-key j select-pane -D
22bind-key k select-pane -U
23bind-key l select-pane -R
24bind-key s source $HOME/.tmux.conf
25bind-key -T copy-mode-vi v send-keys -X begin-selection
26bind-key -T copy-mode-vi r send-keys -X rectangle-toggle
27bind-key P display-popup -E "psysh"
28bind-key T display-popup -w 80% -h 80% -d "#{pane_current_path}" -E $SHELL
29bind-key M display-popup -w 95% -h 95% -E 'bash -i -c "x man"'
30
31# Wayland clipboard
32bind-key -T copy-mode-vi y send-keys -X copy-pipe-and-cancel "wl-copy"
33bind-key ] run "wl-paste | tmux load-buffer - && tmux paste-buffer"
34set -g update-environment "WAYLAND_DISPLAY DISPLAY XAUTHORITY XDG_RUNTIME_DIR"
35
36# Colors
37BG="#f5f5f5"
38FG="#000000"
39RED="#ee0000"
40GRAY="#aaaaaa"
41DARKGRAY="#666666"
42
43# Status bar
44set -g status-style "bg=${BG},fg=${FG}"
45set -g status-left-length 40
46set -g status-right-length 60
47
48set -g status-left "#[fg=${RED},bold]#S #[fg=${DARKGRAY}]│ "
49set -g status-right "#[fg=${DARKGRAY}]%a %d %b #[fg=${RED},bold]%H:%M"
50
51# Windows
52set -g window-status-format "#[fg=${DARKGRAY}] #I:#W "
53set -g window-status-current-format "#[fg=${RED},bold] #I:#W "
54set -g window-status-separator ""
55
56# Pane borders
57set -g pane-border-style "fg=${GRAY}"
58set -g pane-active-border-style "fg=${RED}"
59
60# Messages and command prompt
61set -g message-style "bg=${BG},fg=${RED}"
62set -g message-command-style "bg=${BG},fg=${FG}"
63
64# Copy mode
65set -g mode-style "bg=${RED},fg=${FG}"
66
67# Popups
68set -g popup-border-style "fg=${RED}"
69set -g popup-style "bg=${BG}"

Responsibilities

The configuration defines:

  • Key bindings

  • Session behavior

  • Window and pane management

Integration

tmux is typically initialized via:

  • Scripts (init-tmux.sh)

  • Shells (dev.sh)

Notes

  • fastfetch configurations are experimental and may change frequently.

  • tmux behavior is tightly integrated with the shell environment.
